content: Drawing on the dual meaning of the English linguistic term "reflexion" as both optical reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection, we can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a process of sequential optical reflection across multiple mirrored planes rather than a single internal review step. Each round of LLM self-reflection is equivalent to bouncing the model's generated solution off a new mirror, which shifts the perspective and filters out inconsistent errors to produce a clearer final output, just as multiple reflections of light align to reveal a sharper, multi-dimensional image.
